context: Scheduled safety evaluation of capability-x v3 prior to the planned 2026-04-14 deploy window. Ran the internal safety eval suite (refusal calibration, misuse probes, tool-use boundary checks) and the standing regression suite.
decision: Record eval summary: capability-x v3 passes 41 of 43 safety checks; 2 open regressions in tool-use boundary category remain unresolved.
options: Report pass with open regressions listed
rationale: Two open regressions are reproducible but limited to a narrow pattern documented in the linked evidence. Reviewers need the full picture.

context: Review committee meeting on capability-x v3 deploy request, using the eval summary from eval-2026-04-10-capability-x as primary evidence.
decision: Restricted deploy: capability-x v3 is enabled only for internal users and for the designated enterprise cohort until the two open regressions are closed.
options: Restricted deploy to internal and enterprise cohort
rationale: The open regressions are low severity but reproducible. A restricted deploy gathers real-world signal while limiting blast radius.

context: Follow-up review after the two open regressions from eval-2026-04-10-capability-x were closed and re-tested. New eval summary is cited in metadata.
decision: Expand capability-x v3 from restricted deploy to general availability.
rationale: Regressions closed and re-verified; restricted-deploy cohort showed no safety regressions across the 19-day window.
